title: Amendment in G.S.R. 82 dt. 10.02.2011

n>iifrhH" ^m i> # r e $ w$ *TT»I ^ ^rc 3 fan* 10-2-2011 ^t <raa 3ifl^^^1^sft3«**iwid*i*Tffe THtW(fafo?T)U 1054/2011 ^ ^ R f t ^ ^ # 3 k ^ R # T ^wm? ^ wwr 1^gn-i^Rf ^ ^RR ^R*SR 3 ^ F ^ J fan fig'J|*H ^jfai t*^'-^^m^F^ron,Trcresrfon 3nft 3 ^f#*w ^ ^t ^ " ^ ^ r ^IHRI URT ^t wi.', • *R3>R ^ ^ 1 ^ *TR^ ^»I t^TCHR ^ £H fclfc 6-4-2011 ^ « M aUT^T 3 TFTHt*? fe# ' 3 ^ -m*H*r1«I ^T 1983 GI/2011 (l> 3RT: 3R HFT-iN ftwft " 3 ^ -^IIIW^ ^ (^*llFi^i1 ^ smiK m ^ wmi v^m f^#F 10-2-2011 * ^?t 3rftR^T ^ T l l i f t 82(3T) ^ ?M % ^ *TC W»T (ii) 5ftt*F Vim TFT ^ % ^ 5^<WI1 ^ ^ f 1gr i i m ^ t n ^ %? ^ f e ? n ? « ^ e 3|R %^fei^^RiT3TTT> ncbftd r * ^ - 5 n n # ^ 3 ^ ^ , - ^ ^t " ^ ^ "#, " ^ ^fr * 3fR 3PT WW "^ ^ ^f I'' [tR f i ^3?T-11014/1/2011-^P^T] 3?^II "qnsi, ^T^Ri Hftw MINISTRY OFHEALTHAND FAMILY WELFARE (Department of Health and Family Welfare) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 30thMay, 2011 GS.R, 418(E).—Whereas, the Central Government was satisfied on the basis of the recommendations of an expert sub-committee set up by the Drugs Technical Advisory Board mat use of certain drugs, including the •i*M -- 1 of 2 -- drug Human Placental Extract and its formulations for human use", is likely to involve certain risks to human beuigs and whereas safer alternatives to the said dmes are available; ^ Whereas, the Central Government was satisfied that it is necessary and expedient to prohibit the manufacture, sale and distribution of the said drugs in public interest and accordingly in exercise of the powers conferred by section 26Aofthe Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940(23 of 1940) prohibited the manufacture, sale and distribution of the sad drugs with immediate effect by a notification No. G.S.R. 82(E) dated 10-2-2011; Whereas, the said notification dated 10-2-2011 with respect to its part relating to the drug "Human Placental Extract and its formulations for human use" was challenged in Hie H,gh Court ofDelhi in Writ Petition (Civil) No 1054/ 2011 and during me deliberations before the Hon'ble Court, the Government gave an undertaking that the issues related to safety, efficacy etc. in respect of the drug 'Human Placental Extract' may be examined by a larger committee; Whereas, on the undertaking of the Government the Hon'ble High Court of Delhi in its Order dated 6-4-2011, while disposing off the matter, opined that the ™GAZETTE OFJNDIA: EXTRAORDINARY [PART fi—SEC. 3(ftl decision as to whether the ban is to be sustained or lifted can only be arrived at after the Committee carries out a comprehensive exercise and submit a report in that regard; Now, therefore, on the basis of the recommendations ot the Expert Committee constituted by the Government as per the directions of the Hon'ble High Court of Delhi the Central Government hereby amends item No. (4) of the third paragraph of the aforesaid notification No G SR. 82(E^ dated 10.2.2011 asunder: ' "4. Human Placental Extract and its formulations for human use except its (i) Topical application for wound healing, and (ii) Injection for pelvic inflammatory disease subject to the condition that the manufacturers of Human Placental Extract and its formulations for human use shall maintain meticulous details and traceability of the donors ofthe Placenta so as to ensure that the Placenta is collected from the donors who are free from HIV, HBsAg, HC V and other viruses." [F. No. X-11014/1/2011-DFQC] ARUN K. PANDA, Jt. Secy.
